verses.text: The LORD: sanctify to Me every firstborn, whatever opens the womb among Israel — both of man and beast. It is Mine. Guidance: The night God spared the firstborn, He claimed them. What was saved by grace now belongs to God. Your survival from any disaster is also a consecration.

The LORD: sanctify to Me every firstborn, whatever opens the womb among Israel — both of man and beast. It is Mine. Guidance: The night God spared the firstborn, He claimed them. What was saved by grace now belongs to God. Your survival from any disaster is also a consecration.

Moses tells the people: remember this day you came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out. No leavened bread shall be eaten today. This day in the month of Abib. Guidance: God anchors the memory in an exact date. Your story of rescue should have a calendar entry, not just a feeling.

Moses tells the people: remember this day you came out from Egypt, out of the house of bondage; for by strength of hand the LORD brought you out. No leavened bread shall be eaten today. This day in the month of Abib. Guidance: God anchors the memory in an exact date. Your story of rescue should have a calendar entry, not just a feeling.

When the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Hivites, Jebusites — which He swore to your fathers — a land flowing with milk and honey, you shall keep this service in this month. Guidance: Worship must travel with you into prosperity. The feast that began in slavery is to be kept in the promised land. Don't let comfort cancel the rhythms born in trouble.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; the seventh shall be a feast to the LORD. No leaven shall be seen with you in all your borders. Guidance: God names what is to be both eaten and not seen. Faithfulness is not only the right meal, but the absence of the wrong ingredient.

Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread; the seventh shall be a feast to the LORD. No leaven shall be seen with you in all your borders. Guidance: God names what is to be both eaten and not seen. Faithfulness is not only the right meal, but the absence of the wrong ingredient.

Tell your son: this is done because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt. It shall be a sign upon your hand and a memorial between your eyes. Guidance: The faith of one generation becomes the next generation's inheritance only through the act of telling. Don't assume your child will know what you don't say out loud.

Tell your son: this is done because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt. It shall be a sign upon your hand and a memorial between your eyes. Guidance: The faith of one generation becomes the next generation's inheritance only through the act of telling. Don't assume your child will know what you don't say out loud.

You shall therefore keep this ordinance in its season from year to year. Guidance: Annual repetition is part of how God protects the memory. The thing you commemorate once will fade; the thing you keep every year stays alive.

When the LORD brings you into the land, set apart to the LORD every firstling — male — of beast. The firstling of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or break its neck. Every firstborn son among your children you shall redeem. Guidance: Even the donkey gets redeemed by a lamb. Every life God spared is replaced or ransomed; nothing is taken for granted as if it were ours to keep.

When the LORD brings you into the land, set apart to the LORD every firstling — male — of beast. The firstling of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or break its neck. Every firstborn son among your children you shall redeem. Guidance: Even the donkey gets redeemed by a lamb. Every life God spared is replaced or ransomed; nothing is taken for granted as if it were ours to keep.

When the LORD brings you into the land, set apart to the LORD every firstling — male — of beast. The firstling of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, or break its neck. Every firstborn son among your children you shall redeem. Guidance: Even the donkey gets redeemed by a lamb. Every life God spared is replaced or ransomed; nothing is taken for granted as if it were ours to keep.

When your son asks, what is this? — say: by strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt. When Pharaoh would hardly let us go, the LORD slew the firstborn of Egypt. Therefore I sacrifice every male that opens the womb, but the firstborn of my children I redeem. Guidance: Your child's questions are an invitation to teach. The answer is always a story — what happened, what God did, what we now do.

When your son asks, what is this? — say: by strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt. When Pharaoh would hardly let us go, the LORD slew the firstborn of Egypt. Therefore I sacrifice every male that opens the womb, but the firstborn of my children I redeem. Guidance: Your child's questions are an invitation to teach. The answer is always a story — what happened, what God did, what we now do.

It shall be a sign on your hand and frontlets between your eyes: for by strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt. Guidance: God ties memory to the body. What you remember should be carried so visibly that your hands and your face look different because of it.

When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them through the land of the Philistines, though it was near. He said: lest the people repent when they see war, and return to Egypt. So God led the people around by the wilderness of the Red Sea. Guidance: God's longer way is often the kinder way. The route that looks shortest may be the one your faith can't yet survive.

When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them through the land of the Philistines, though it was near. He said: lest the people repent when they see war, and return to Egypt. So God led the people around by the wilderness of the Red Sea. Guidance: God's longer way is often the kinder way. The route that looks shortest may be the one your faith can't yet survive.

Moses took the bones of Joseph with him, because Joseph had made the children of Israel solemnly swear: God will surely visit you; you shall carry my bones up with you. Guidance: Four hundred years later, the oath is kept. Faith you live by today may have to be fulfilled by your great-great-grandchildren. Live worth that kind of remembering.

They journeyed from Succoth and camped at Etham, on the edge of the wilderness. Guidance: The first day out of slavery still ends at a campsite, not at home. Don't expect freedom to feel like arrival on its first night.

The LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire — to give light, that they might travel day or night. He did not take away the pillar of cloud by day, nor the pillar of fire by night. Guidance: God's presence took a form they could not miss. Notice the constancy — what guides you in the bright hour also burns through your night.
